name: acquisition writes rules extracted from cited source content into the per-project working overlay with provenance
command: ID=P556Q01; bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/commands/acquire-technology-rules.sh" --technology Vue --project $ID --source-file g.txt --source-id sid --source-url "https://vuejs.org/style-guide/" --now 2026-07-05T00:00:00Z 2>e.err; F=$(ls "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/generated-code-quality-standards"/_project/$ID/vue/*.yaml 2>/dev/null | head -1); ok=no; [ -n "$F" ] && ruby -ryaml -e 'd=YAML.unsafe_load_file(ARGV[0]); exit(d["source"]["authoritative_url"].include?("vuejs.org") && d["rules"].length>=1 ? 0:1)' "$F" && ok=yes; rm -rf "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/generated-code-quality-standards"/_project/$ID; [ "$ok" = yes ] && echo acquire-writes-cited 1>&2
setup: printf '%s\n' 'Always bind a unique key to every loop element.' 'Never combine conditional and loop on one element.' > g.txt
